title: The TesTly AI Assistant
subtitle: A smart helper you chat with to find labs and tests
intro: TesTly is an online marketplace for medical lab tests in Egypt. The AI Assistant is a chat box inside the app: you type a normal question, and it finds real labs and tests for you.
bullets: Ask in plain words, like texting a knowledgeable friend
It shows real labs and tests you can tap and book
Built to be honest: it never makes up prices or fake labs

title: What It Is, In Plain Words
intro: Two different helpers live inside TesTly. They look similar but work very differently.

cards.name: The AI Health Assistant
cards.what: A chat helper that understands plain questions like 'I need a cheap blood sugar test near me' and replies in friendly language while showing real matching labs and tests.
cards.why: Patients do not know test names or lab prices; letting them just ask in their own words is far easier than browsing menus.

cards.name: The Guided FAQ Bot
cards.what: A small pop-up helper that answers common questions (like 'how do I fast before a test?') by matching keywords to a short, hand-written list of answers.
cards.why: Common questions do not need expensive AI; a simple, predictable bot answers them instantly and for free.

title: Tech Stack & Why
intro: The pieces we used to build the AI Assistant, and why each one was the right choice.

cards.name: Google Gemini
cards.what: Gemini is Google's AI that reads a question and writes a human-like answer. We use the model called 'gemini-2.5-flash'.
cards.why: The 'flash' version is fast and cheap, which suits a chat that must reply quickly and runs on a student-project budget.

cards.name: @google/genai SDK
cards.what: A ready-made code library that lets our server talk to Gemini without us writing the low-level networking by hand.
cards.why: It is Google's official, up-to-date toolkit, so it supports the newest features like tool calling and live streaming out of the box.

cards.name: Function Calling (Tools)
cards.what: A feature where the AI can ask our server to run a specific job, like 'look up labs for a blood test', instead of guessing the answer.
cards.why: This is what keeps the assistant honest: it pulls real labs and prices from our database instead of inventing them.

cards.name: Server-Sent Events (SSE)
cards.what: A simple way for our server to keep sending pieces of the answer to your screen as they are ready, instead of waiting for the whole reply.
cards.why: You see the answer type out word-by-word and cards pop in instantly, so the chat feels alive and fast instead of frozen.

title: Key Tools the AI Can Use
intro: We gave the AI just two 'tools' (jobs it can ask our server to run). We kept the list tiny on purpose.

rows.name: find_labs
rows.what: Finds real labs that offer a given test, with their price, address, and rating. You can optionally narrow it to one city.
rows.why: Answers 'where can I get this test?' and 'which lab is cheapest?' using only real, approved labs from our database.

rows.name: search_tests
rows.what: Looks up tests in our catalog and shows the lowest price and how many labs offer each one.
rows.why: Answers 'what does this test cost?' or 'what tests exist?' so a patient can compare before booking.

rows.name: Two tools only (by design)
rows.what: We deliberately gave the AI a very small, clear set of tools rather than many complicated ones.
rows.why: A short, simple menu makes the AI far more reliable at picking the right tool and filling it in correctly.

title: How a Chat Works (Big Picture)
intro: What happens from the moment you press send to the moment you see lab cards.
imageCaption: Your message travels to our server, the server works with Gemini and the database, then streams the answer back.

steps.title: Step 1: You send a message
steps.detail: You type something like 'where can I get a CBC in Cairo?' and the app sends it to our server over a live connection that stays open for the reply.

steps.title: Step 2: The server runs the show
steps.detail: Our own server (not your phone) talks to Gemini. This keeps the secret AI key and the database safely on our side, away from the user's device.

steps.title: Step 3: The answer streams back live
steps.detail: As Gemini writes, the server forwards each piece to your screen right away, and lab/test cards appear the instant a lookup finishes.

steps.title: Step 4: The chat is saved
steps.detail: Both your question and the assistant's reply (including the cards) are stored, so reopening the conversation shows everything again exactly as before.

title: The Main Algorithm: The Tool-Calling Loop
intro: The clever part. Instead of the AI guessing about labs and prices, it asks our server to look up real data, then writes its answer from that. This back-and-forth is called the 'function-calling loop'.

steps.title: 1. You send a message
steps.detail: Your plain-language question, like 'I need a cheap blood sugar test near me', goes to the backend server.

steps.title: 2. Server hands Gemini your message PLUS a toolbox
steps.detail: The server passes your question to Gemini along with the list of tools it is allowed to use: find_labs and search_tests.

steps.title: 3. Gemini decides to call a tool
steps.detail: Gemini reads your question and realizes it needs real data, so instead of answering it says 'please run find_labs for a glucose test'. This is what 'function calling' means: the AI asks for a job to be done.

steps.title: 4. The server runs the tool on real data
steps.detail: The backend runs that lookup against the live database, returning only labs and tests that are active and approved, with their true prices and ratings.

steps.title: 5. Results go back to Gemini
steps.detail: The server sends the found labs back to Gemini so it now has real facts to talk about, not guesses.

steps.title: 6. Gemini writes a friendly answer
steps.detail: Now grounded in real data, Gemini writes a short, natural reply, for example 'I found 3 labs offering a glucose test, starting from 80 EGP. See the cards below.'

steps.title: 7. The app shows the answer plus clickable cards
steps.detail: Your screen shows the friendly text and the lab/test cards. Tapping a card opens that lab or test so you can book it.

why: This loop is the heart of the assistant: it lets the AI talk naturally while every fact comes from our real catalog, not its imagination.

title: Why a Loop, Not Just One Answer
intro: Sometimes one lookup is not enough, or the AI must look something up before it can reply. So the server repeats the conversation with Gemini until the answer is ready.

steps.title: Round by round
steps.detail: Each round, Gemini either calls a tool or writes its final answer. If it calls a tool, the server runs it and starts another round with the new results.

steps.title: A safety limit of 4 rounds
steps.detail: The loop is capped at four rounds. This stops a confused AI from looping forever and keeps replies fast and cheap.

steps.title: Cards appear before the text
steps.detail: Because the server runs the loop itself, it can show you the lab cards the moment a lookup finishes, even while the friendly summary is still being written.

why: Running the loop on our server (not the user's device) means we control the cost, the speed limit, and the safety rules in one trusted place.

title: The Guardrails: Keeping a Health AI Safe
intro: AI can sometimes 'hallucinate' (confidently make things up). In a health and money app that is dangerous, so we built strict rules into the assistant.

steps.title: A strict rulebook (system prompt)
steps.detail: Before every chat, we give Gemini a fixed set of instructions it must follow, describing exactly what it may and may not do.

steps.title: Never invent prices or labs
steps.detail: The rules forbid making up any lab, price, or availability. Real numbers may come only from the tools that read the real database.

steps.title: Never act like a doctor
steps.detail: The assistant is told it is not a doctor: it must not diagnose illnesses or read a specific person's results, and it must tell users with emergency symptoms to seek care immediately.

steps.title: Only real catalog data
steps.detail: It can explain what a test is in general terms, but anything specific (which lab, what price) must come from a real lookup, not a guess.

why: In a health app, a made-up price or a wrong medical claim could harm or mislead a patient, so honesty is not optional, it is a core feature.

title: The FAQ Bot: Simple On Purpose
intro: The second, simpler helper. It uses no AI at all, and that is exactly the point.

cards.name: How it works
cards.what: It compares your question to a short, hand-written list of common questions and answers stored in the database, then returns the closest match.
cards.why: It is fully predictable: the same question always gives the same trusted answer, with no chance of the AI making something up.

cards.name: What it handles
cards.what: Things like fasting before a test, result timing, booking help, and privacy, plus quick-tap topic buttons.
cards.why: These questions repeat constantly, so a fixed answer is faster and friendlier than asking the AI every time.

cards.name: Why have both
cards.what: Simple, repeating questions go to the free FAQ bot; open-ended, catalog-aware questions go to the AI assistant.
cards.why: We save money and avoid risk on easy questions, and spend the AI's power only where it truly adds value.

title: Putting It All Together
imageCaption: The AI Assistant is one part of TesTly: the web and mobile apps talk to one server, which talks to the database and to Google Gemini.
bullets: You chat in plain words; the assistant finds real labs and tests, not made-up ones
Gemini does the talking, but our database provides every real fact via tool calls
Live streaming makes replies appear word-by-word with instant clickable cards
Strict guardrails keep it honest: no fake prices, no medical diagnoses
A simple no-AI FAQ bot handles common questions, saving the AI for the hard ones
